test_sufficiency_summary: 13 new tests provide adequate coverage of all acceptance criteria. AC1 (all five headers, CSP enforced not report-only, all-routes pattern), AC4 (per-allowance dev/prod variants), and AC5 (no HSTS on web) are directly asserted in next.config.spec.ts. AC2 (helmet registered before routing) is covered by invocation-order assertion in index.test.ts. AC5 for the API is verified indirectly via bootstrap-success and structural audit. No test directly asserts strictTransportSecurity:false option value (accepted mock-architecture limitation, N1). Test sufficiency is adequate.
documentation_sufficiency_summary: docs/features/web-shell.md and docs/development/api-conventions.md are accurate and complete. docs/operations/deployment.md is missing the explicit HSTS enforcement-point statement required by the plan (W2). The omission creates a minor operational documentation gap but does not misrepresent any security property.
regression_assessment: No new XSS, CSRF, or clickjacking vectors introduced. All unsafe-inline allowances are pre-existing Next.js 15 constraints. Dev-only connect-src is environment-gated. Helmet defaults add protection; only HSTS and CSP are disabled, both deliberate and documented.

warnings.summary: style-src 'unsafe-inline' lacks an in-code justification comment (AC4 partial gap). No security regression; justification exists in implementer report and web-shell.md but not in the source file alongside the directive.
Deployment runbook not updated with explicit HSTS enforcement-point statement per plan scope. HSTS rationale is captured in web-shell.md and api-conventions.md but not in the operational runbook.
